title: Statutory Clock Escalation Guide
content: A statutory clock is a legally mandated deadline such as breach notification or recall reporting. RouterExecutor treats statutory-clock indicators as SEV1-forcing regardless of other severity indicators. The incident must escalate to a human queue, and ActionAgent must not approve waivers or suppress escalation. Sitreps should cite the originating signal, the applicable compliance guide, and any incident record showing clock start time. If the system does not know which statutory clock applies, it says so and escalates.
snippet: Compliance guide for statutory clocks, SEV1 forcing, escalation, and citation requirements.

title: NFIRS / NIBRS Sitrep Source Checklist
content: For NFIRS / NIBRS reporting, the sitrep should include only cited facts: incident id, date and time received, location, incident type, responding unit status, known injuries or property impact, and disposition. All factual claims must cite source records such as the incident record, signal text, field unit status update, or approved SOP. Do not infer cause, suspect, casualty count, or loss estimate without a source record. Missing required facts should be listed as unknown and escalated to compliance-desk.
snippet: Checklist for citation-grounded NFIRS / NIBRS sitrep facts and unknown handling.

title: Recall / Compliance Incident Reporting Procedure
content: Recall and compliance signals may start a statutory clock when they mention regulated product defects, public notice deadlines, safety reports, or agency reporting windows. Open the incident in compliance-desk, preserve every signal as a report, and attach customer-comms templates only after compliance approves public language. SEV1 applies when a statutory clock is active or life safety is implicated. The sitrep must cite the signal, incident record, and approved compliance source; no model-generated legal conclusion is allowed.
snippet: Procedure for recall/compliance reporting with statutory-clock and public-language controls.
